beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, and morning routine at home.
beats.reasoning: Aiden wakes early as always—the pre-dawn drive to Macrina is his routine. Low energy yesterday (40/100) means a quiet, focused morning before work.

beats.activity: Drive to Macrina Bakery in Kirkland.
beats.reasoning: Transit strike all day until 4pm, so Aiden must drive. Budget 45 min for commute from Roosevelt to Kirkland with possible surge-pricing traffic.

beats.activity: Work: laminating croissants, pain au chocolat, and managing morning service.
beats.reasoning: Core pastry chef shift—lamination work is meditative for Aiden, especially after yesterday's exhaustion. Partly cloudy weather means steady foot traffic.

beats.activity: Lunch break in the bakery break room.
beats.reasoning: Aiden typically eats in the break room—quick, familiar, lets him decompress between morning and afternoon service.

beats.activity: Work: afternoon service and closing duties.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon shift through close. Transit strike ends at 4pm but Aiden is already at work, so no impact on his schedule.
Transit strike now lifted, but Aiden prefers to drive. 50 min buffer for post-strike traffic and end-of-day congestion on I-405 and I-5.

beats.activity: Quick errand: pick up groceries and wine at QFC.
beats.reasoning: Neighborhood errand on the way home—10 min walk/shop. Aiden likes to cook slowly and intentionally after work, continuing the pattern from recent days.

beats.activity: Prep and cook dinner at home.
beats.reasoning: Aiden cooks deliberately in the evening—part of his wind-down ritual. The mild, partly cloudy weather means he might step outside with his camera after.

beats.activity: Take camera walk around the neighborhood; capture evening light.; Relax at home: review photos, video games, and wind down.
beats.reasoning: Aiden carries his film camera everywhere and loves shooting golden hour. Partly cloudy skies are ideal, and a neighborhood walk lets him stay close to home given yesterday's low energy.
7-hour sleep window ending at 6am—Aiden's early wake-up routine begins again tomorrow. Already at home, no commute before bed.

diary: Woke up early as always, coffee tasting better than usual—maybe it's the partly cloudy light coming through the kitchen window. The transit strike meant I had to drive to Macrina, but honestly? I didn't mind. Solitude before the morning rush. Spent the early hours laminating croissants, that meditative rhythm of butter and dough. The lunch break in the break room was quiet; I sat alone with my sandwich and watched the city traffic through the small window. Afternoon service flew by. Got home around 2:45, grabbed wine and groceries at QFC—simple stuff. Made a decent dinner, nothing fancy. But the best part was the camera walk at dusk. Roosevelt looked golden, almost painterly. The light hit the old brick buildings just right. Came home, scrolled through the photos, played some games to decompress. Feeling calm tonight, satisfied in that small way you get when a day just... fits.
thought_process: The transit strike initially felt like a disruption, but driving solo gave me unexpected breathing room before the bakery chaos. I'm usually reactive to these city disruptions, but today I leaned into it—took the scenic route through Kirkland, gave myself time. Work was standard, repetitive in the good way. By afternoon I was tired but not burnt out. The errand and dinner prep felt grounding rather than obligatory. I think the real shift happened on that camera walk. I've been meaning to get out with my camera more, and the evening light was genuinely special. It reminded me why I chose to live in Roosevelt—there's beauty here if you slow down enough to see it. The games afterward weren't escapism so much as decompression. I ended the day feeling present instead of drained, which doesn't happen as often as I'd like.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
